verdict_reasoning: SRC (p60-Src) is canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-myristoylation and a polybasic cluster — it is not surface-accessible at baseline. However, a 2026 Science study (autophagolysosomal exocytosis, ALE) demonstrates that in cancer cells, Src undergoes topological inversion and is displayed on the outer leaflet as extracellular Src (eSrc), detected on cancer cell lines, xenografts, and patient tumors, and proposed as an antibody target. This is a bona fide cancer-state-induced outer-leaflet pool, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced.
